meta.description: Practice linked lists in Python through node creation, traversal, search, insertion, deletion, reverse, middle node, cycle detection, merging sorted lists, and removing the nth node from the end.

ui.footer_note: Programmers Picnic AI-ML Classes by Champak Roy

questions.title: Build and Display a Linked List
questions.prompt: Create a singly linked list from a Python list and return its elements as a normal Python list. This teaches how nodes are connected through next pointers.
hints: A linked list starts with head.
Each node stores data and next.
Keep a tail pointer while building the list.
Traverse until current becomes None.
questions.starter_code:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None


def build_linked_list(values):
    head = None
    tail = None
    # TODO: create nodes and connect them
    return head


def to_list(head):
    result = []
    # TODO: traverse and collect data
    return result


head = build_linked_list([10, 20, 30])
print(to_list(head))
print(to_list(build_linked_list([])))

questions.title: Count Nodes in a Linked List
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the number of nodes in the list.
hints: Start count from 0.
Move current one node at a time.
Stop when current becomes None.

questions.title: Search a Value in a Linked List
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list and a target value, return True if the target exists in the list. Otherwise return False.
hints: Check current.data at every node.
Return True immediately when found.
Return False only after the loop ends.

questions.title: Insert at Beginning and End
questions.prompt: Write two functions: insert_beginning(head, value) and insert_end(head, value). Each function should return the updated head of the linked list.
hints: For beginning insertion, the new node becomes the new head.
For end insertion, travel to the last node.
If the list is empty, the new node is also the head.

questions.title: Delete the First Occurrence of a Value
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list and a target value, delete the first node whose data equals the target. Return the updated head.
hints: Deleting the head is a special case.
Keep previous and current pointers.
To delete current, set previous.next = current.next.

<g transform="translate(80,130)">

questions.title: Reverse a Linked List
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hints: Use three pointers: previous, current, next_node.
Save current.next before changing it.
Point current.next backward.
At the end, previous is the new head.

questions.title: Find the Middle Node
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the data of the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
hints: Use a slow pointer and a fast pointer.
Slow moves one step. Fast moves two steps.
When fast reaches the end, slow is at the middle.

questions.title: Detect a Cycle in a Linked List
questions.prompt: Given the head of a linked list, return True if the list contains a cycle. Otherwise return False.
hints: Use Floyd cycle detection.
Slow moves one step. Fast moves two steps.
If slow and fast meet, there is a cycle.
If fast reaches None, there is no cycle.
questions.svg_html:
<svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Detect a Cycle in a Linked List">
<defs><linearGradient id="g2148" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#fed7aa"/></linearGradient></defs>

<g transform="translate(80,130)">

questions.title: Merge Two Sorted Linked Lists
questions.prompt: Given the heads of two sorted singly linked lists, merge them into one sorted linked list and return the new head.
hints: Use a dummy node to simplify the code.
Compare the current nodes of both lists.
Attach the smaller node to the merged list.
Attach the remaining part when one list ends.

questions.title: Remove the Nth Node from the End
questions.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list and an integer n, remove the nth node from the end of the list. Return the updated head.
hints: Use a dummy node before head.
Move fast n steps ahead.
Move slow and fast together until fast reaches the last node.
Delete slow.next.
